commit.message:
fix(emit_code): use forward slashes in path deps for Windows TOML compat

On Windows, PathBuf::display() produces backslash-separated paths
(e.g. D:\a\...\crates\elicitation). TOML treats backslash as an
escape character, making the generated Cargo.toml unparseable.

Fix: replace backslashes with forward slashes after converting to
string. Cargo accepts forward-slash paths on all platforms.

Fixes Windows CI smoke test failures (all 15 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Event lifecycle: cancel+refund, escrow payout, force-delete; E2E cleanup

- POST /events/{id}/cancel — VenueAdmin (own venue) or AppOwner; refunds all
  Paid orders via IPaymentProvider.RefundAsync, voids Reserved orders, marks
  IsCancelled=true. MockPaymentProvider.RefundAsync is a no-op; Stripe
  implementation calls RefundService.CreateAsync.
- POST /events/{id}/release-funds — AppOwner only, requires endsAt < now;
  sets FundsReleasedAt (escrow model: funds held until after event ends).
- DELETE /events/{id}?force=true — AppOwner only; bypasses sold-ticket check
  for test/dev cleanup of mock-payment data.
- VenueAdmins can only delete/cancel their own venue's events; AppOwner can
  act on any event.
- EF migration AddEventCancellationAndPayout: IsCancelled, CancelledAt,
  CancellationReason, FundsReleasedAt columns on Events table.
- Frontend: cancelled banner + disabled buy/qty on event detail page;
  venue dashboard shows 'Cancelled' badge, 'Pending payout' / 'Funds released'
  indicator per event after it ends.
- E2E: deleteEvent helper now uses owner force=true so afterAll cleanup always
  succeeds regardless of ticket sales or order status.
- All describe blocks that create events now have afterAll cleanup.
- live() directive fix for type/date filter selects in page-events.ts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: simplify toolExecutor enforcement for MCP architecture (Step 7)

Remove grant check and scope check from toolExecutor.ts — these are now
handled by Entra agent identities and MCP server-side scoping.

Enforcement pipeline simplified from 5 layers to 4:
1. Emergency block check (is_blocked in agent_tool_grants)
2. Rate limit check (60 calls/hr default)
3. Budget check (per-run, daily, monthly caps)
4. Execute + timeout

Changes:
- Replace grant cache with block cache (isToolBlocked)
- Remove toolGrants map and registerGrants() method
- Remove auto-grant/auto-sync logic (no longer needed)
- Remove scope check (wildcard pattern matching)
- Add is_blocked column migration for agent_tool_grants
- Keep legacy exports (isToolGranted, invalidateGrantCache) as
  deprecated wrappers for backward compatibility
- Export new isToolBlocked and invalidateBlockCache from index.ts

commit.message:
fix(seo): fix JSON-LD, Twitter card and OGP meta tag issues

- jsonld.ftl: mainEntityOfPage now points to article URL (content.uri),
  not the site homepage; fixes Google's schema-to-page association
- jsonld.ftl: add description field from content.description to schema
- jsonld.ftl: remove articleBody (unreliable regex HTML-stripping, page bloat)
- jsonld.ftl: default featured image and publisher logo URLs changed from
  relative (rootpath prefix) to absolute (site_host prefix); relative URLs
  are invalid in JSON-LD and Google will reject them
- jsonld.ftl: fix missing / separator between site_host and content.uri in url field
- header.ftl: twitter:image and twitter:image:alt changed from property= to
  name= (Twitter Card spec requires name=, not property=; property= is OGP-only)
- header.ftl: add og:image:alt and twitter:image:alt alongside every og:image
  (accessibility + OGP best practice)
- header.ftl: default image fallback now also emits twitter:image + alt tags
- header.ftl: default og:image now uses absolute URL (site_host prefix)
- header.ftl: remove profile:first_name/profile:last_name meta from post/page
  types; these profile: namespace properties are only valid with og:type=profile,
  not og:type=article; emitting them on article pages is a spec violation

commit.message:
Supplement hit test with bounds-based tree walk

GetVisualTreeElements uses parent-relative Frame bounds for hit testing,
which fails for nested elements (e.g. Labels inside Border) on platforms
like macOS AppKit where GetBoundingBox returns view.Frame instead of
window-absolute coordinates.

Added HitTestByBounds which walks the visual tree and checks
ResolveWindowBounds (window-absolute coords) for each element. Results
are merged with GetVisualTreeElements, deduplicating by reference.

commit.message:
FIX: Add missing get_recent_logs() and clear_log() methods to EFS_Error_Handler

PROBLEM:
- Dashboard controller called get_recent_logs() method that didn't exist in EFS_Error_Handler
- This caused fatal error on website admin page (but tests passed because they don't load dashboard)
- clear_log() method was also missing (needed by logs AJAX handler)

SOLUTION:
Added two critical methods to EFS_Error_Handler:

1. get_recent_logs()
   - Retrieves migration logs from 'efs_migration_log' WP option
   - Returns array of log entries (empty array if none exist)
   - Used by Dashboard Controller to populate admin interface context

2. clear_log()
   - Deletes the 'efs_migration_log' WP option
   - Returns bool success status
   - Used by Logs AJAX handler for clearing migration logs

Both methods follow the existing pattern in the class and are properly documented.

VERIFICATION:
✅ 165/165 unit tests PASS
✅ Website loads without fatal errors
✅ Dashboard admin page accessible
✅ No regressions from previous fixes

This resolves the critical website error that appeared after EFS_Error_Handler consolidation in Phase 7.
